title: bridge_anomaly sentinel → atlas_health_timeline JSONL sibling row
summary: Promote __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ from a stdout-only sentinel into a structured time-series record. Modify the ω-bridge-15 invocation block in tool/bridge_health.sh: capture analyzer stdout (instead of suppressing it), re-emit the sentinel verbatim (preserves operator-visible behaviour), then parse the positional sentinel fields (verdict / bridges / samples / flagged / window / mult) via single awk substitution and append a sibling JSONL line scoped 'bridge_anomaly' to $TIMELINE. Gated under JSON=0 so the existing --json mode still suppresses timeline writes (consistent with the bridge_registry line at L341-343).

predecessors.omega_bridge_15: established the CLI-only anomaly invocation; this cycle adds structured time-series persistence for the result
predecessors.atlas_health_timeline_jsonl_pattern: single-line-per-event JSONL with scope= discriminator — re-used here without changing the existing scope='bridge_registry' line shape

context.problem: Anomaly verdicts were printed to stdout but never written to disk. Time-series tooling (anomaly rate over rolling window, MTBF per bridge, alert thresholds) had no structured record to query — operators would need to re-grep the analyzer's sentinel from terminal logs after each sweep.
context.design_goal: Zero new files. Reuse $TIMELINE. Reuse the 'one JSONL line per scope per event' pattern. No new schema-discovery cost for downstream consumers — they pivot on the scope field.

changes: verbatim re-emit: printf '%s\n' "$ANOMALY_OUT" preserves prior operator-visible behaviour
positional awk parser: extracts verdict + 5 numeric fields by stripping `key=` prefix from $3..$7
JSONL append: gated on JSON=0 (parity with bridge_registry line); writes to $TIMELINE
robustness: empty ANOMALY_OUT short-circuits; missing __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ line short-circuits; tolerates trailing reason=/path= fields in INSUFFICIENT_DATA verdict (positional parse stops at $7)

validation.input: __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ WARN bridges=2 samples=9 flagged=2 window=7 mult=3.0
validation.output: valid JSON, all fields populated correctly

validation.step: isolated parser, INSUFFICIENT_DATA with trailing fields
validation.input: __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ INSUFFICIENT_DATA bridges=0 samples=0 flagged=0 window=7 mult=3.0 reason=history_missing path=/x/y/z.tsv
validation.output: valid JSON, trailing reason/path correctly ignored by positional parse

scope_constraints_honoured: minimal blast — single block edit in one tracked .sh
raw 77 append-only — sibling JSONL row per sweep, never rewrites prior rows
raw 80 sentinel preserved — analyzer's __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ still emitted to stdout verbatim
raw 71 report-only — analyzer failure stays non-fatal; main exit code untouched
CLI-only invocation preserved — no library/path coupling change vs bridge-15

no parallel-session collision — only tracked tool/bridge_health.sh edited; cli/run.hexa, n6/*, kick_*, atlas_health_timeline.jsonl all untouched (the JSONL is APPENDED to at runtime, not edited at source)
no commit in anima — closure JSON written as untracked

non_changes.atlas_health_timeline.jsonl: currently dirty (parallel-session) — NOT touched by this cycle (the script appends at runtime; my edit only modifies the .sh that does the appending)
non_changes.tool/bridge_anomaly.hexa: untouched — analyzer remains the source of truth, this cycle only reshapes the consumer side

next_followups: downstream alert rule: tail -F atlas_health_timeline.jsonl | jq 'select(.scope=="bridge_anomaly" and .verdict=="WARN")' for live alerting
consider exposing per-bridge flag breakdown (currently aggregated as flagged=N) — would need analyzer to also emit per-flag JSONL or extend the sentinel
